author: rādhāmohana dāsa
verses:
śrī⋅guru⋅vaiṣṇava, tomāra caraṇa,
smaraṇa nā kainu āmi
viṣaya viṣama, viṣa bhāla māni,
khāichu hoiyā kāmī

sei viṣe more, jāriyā mārila,
baḍoi viṣama haila
janame janame, emana katoi,
ātmaghātī pāpa kaila

sei aparādhe, e bhava⋅saṁsāre,
bā̐dhile e māyājāle
tomā’ nā bhajiyā, āpanā khāiyā,
āpani ḍubechi hele

āra kata kāla, e duḥkha bhuñjiba,
bhoga⋅deha nāhi jāya
sahite nāriyā, kātara hoiyā,
nivedichi tuyā pāya

o rāṅgā caraṇa, paraśa kevala,
vicāriyā ei dāya
uddhāra kariyā, laha dīna-bandhu,
āpana caraṇa⋅nāya

tomāra sevana⋅, amṛta bhojana,
karāiyā more rākha
e rādhāmohana, khate vikāila,
dāma gagane lekha
en_translation:
O Śrī Guru and Vaiṣṇava, I did not remember your feet. I considered the dangerous poison of sense objects as good, and being lusty, I consumed it.

That poison burned me and killed me; it became very severe. In birth after birth, how much such self-killing sin have I committed!

For that offense, in this material world, I was bound in this net of illusion. Not worshipping you, devouring myself, I have drowned myself with ease.

How much longer shall I suffer this misery? The body for enjoyment does not go away. Unable to tolerate, being distressed, I am submitting at your feet.

Only the touch of those reddish feet—considering this crisis, delivering me, take me, O Friend of the Poor, on the boat of your feet.

Your service is eating nectar; make me do it and keep me. This Rādhāmohana is sold by a written contract; write the price in the sky.
